candidate_score_status_reason: 2026-07-09 oracle fairness surgery removed 2 scored tests that asserted unpromised CLI process exit codes (kept 53 -> 51). The shipped score_result.json is the untouched 2026-07-03 WSL run against the 53-test oracle (47/53) and must be rerun against the 51-test oracle by a real candidate agent before use in analysis.

reference_gate.caveat: The 2 residual reference failures (save_out/getargs propagation and dumpdb iteration) are environment artifacts of the scoring venv's Python lacking the _gdbm module, so doit's dependency DB falls back to dbm.ndbm which does not support iteration; the candidate run in the same family of environments passed both, and the superseded hand-written 53/53 reference (archived) also passed them under a gdbm-capable interpreter. These nodeids must not be treated as reference-unsolvable.

notes: 2026-07-09 fairness surgery: removed generated_tests.py::test_info_reports_missing_target_as_reason_to_run (asserted returncode == 1 for `info` with a missing target) and generated_tests.py::test_invalid_task_dictionary_returns_command_error_without_running (asserted returncode == 3) because the spec documents what `info` prints and which error classes report invalid task definitions but never promises CLI process exit codes. Pre-surgery bookkeeping snapshots live in archive/superseded-20260709/doit-taskrunner-oracle53/.

The superseded 53/53 reference_score.json was a hand-written summary (non-harness schema, 'pytest-base with PYTHONPATH reference repo'); it is archived at archive/superseded-20260709/doit-taskrunner-oracle53/tasks-002__reference_score_53_handwritten.json and replaced by the 2026-07-09 harness-run WSL reference.

doit-taskrunner-fullrepro-001 and -002 remain dual-mounted in tasks/ with identical oracles and scores; the reopen protocol says -001 should be retired. Dual-mount de-duplication is tracked as an open item and both directories received identical surgery to stay consistent.

This directory is the dual-mounted predecessor of doit-taskrunner-fullrepro-002 (same oracle, same candidate, same score). Per the reopen protocol it is slated for retirement; kept in sync until the dual-mount open item is resolved so no stale artifacts ship either way.
